Sense.Definition: (indicates a guess at the amount or extent of something)
(emphatic particle used with great or large quantities, sometimes indicating surprise)
(applied to a noun representing a group, marks the subject of a sentence)
(marks the source of something--where something is extracted/taken out of/from)
(marks the basis for doing something--a metaphorical 'place of departure')
he or she (formal third person pronoun)
this (something close to the speaker or that the speaker thinks of)
(with an infinitive, indicates that an action/state/state change is continuing/progressing—typically away from the present)
(marks an action as completed, whether out of relief or regret)
that (something close to the listener or that the listener thinks of)
(indicates a past completed event with present relevance)
(past perfective indicator with implication of not being true at the present time)
this (something close to the speaker or that the speaker thinks of)
you (singular; used politely in familiar situations)
(friendly greeting both in meeting and in parting)
옛날의 의식이나 법식을 따르는, 또는 그런 것
고전이 될 만한 내용과 의의를 가지는, 또는 그런 것
(causative connective, where what precedes it is the purpose/cause/result/etc. of what follows it)
(marks the possessor in a possessive construction)
(marks the (generally spatial) direction in which something happens)
(marks an object (generally inanimate) of a comparison)
(marks something being added to something else)
(in a number of fixed expressions, marks something being talked about)
(infinitive marker; used to connect verbs with certain following auxiliaries)
(marks the location in/on which something is done)
(indicator of a future event, based on present information or subjective belief)
he or she (informal third person pronoun)
(honorific used in deference to the topic of the statement)
(marks the animate target of a conveyance-like action)
(marker of the complement of 되다 and 아니다)
(marks the location in/on which something is done)
(marks the animate target of a conveyance-like action)
(indicates a sequential relationship between two events)
(indicates the ways/means in which another event occurs)

Synset.Definition: (indicates a guess at the amount or extent of something)
(emphatic particle used with great or large quantities, sometimes indicating surprise)
(applied to a noun representing a group, marks the subject of a sentence)
(marks the source of something--where something is extracted/taken out of/from)
(marks the basis for doing something--a metaphorical 'place of departure')
he or she (formal third person pronoun)
this (something close to the speaker or that the speaker thinks of)
(with an infinitive, indicates that an action/state/state change is continuing/progressing—typically away from the present)
(marks an action as completed, whether out of relief or regret)
that (something close to the listener or that the listener thinks of)
(indicates a past completed event with present relevance)
(past perfective indicator with implication of not being true at the present time)
this (something close to the speaker or that the speaker thinks of)
you (singular; used politely in familiar situations)
(friendly greeting both in meeting and in parting)
옛날의 의식이나 법식을 따르는, 또는 그런 것
고전이 될 만한 내용과 의의를 가지는, 또는 그런 것
(causative connective, where what precedes it is the purpose/cause/result/etc. of what follows it)
(marks the possessor in a possessive construction)
(marks the (generally spatial) direction in which something happens)
(marks an object (generally inanimate) of a comparison)
(marks something being added to something else)
(in a number of fixed expressions, marks something being talked about)
(infinitive marker; used to connect verbs with certain following auxiliaries)
(marks the location in/on which something is done)
(indicator of a future event, based on present information or subjective belief)
he or she (informal third person pronoun)
(honorific used in deference to the topic of the statement)
(marks the animate target of a conveyance-like action)
(marker of the complement of 되다 and 아니다)
(marks the location in/on which something is done)
(marks the animate target of a conveyance-like action)
(indicates a sequential relationship between two events)
(indicates the ways/means in which another event occurs)
